title: My Price Reflects My Value, Not My Wobble
subtitle: An energy statement for releasing fear-based underpricing and aligning with the full worth of your work
excerpt: A short energy statement for the gap between the price you want to charge and the price you actually say. The release names the fear that drives the gap; the allow names a pricing practice grounded in value rather than in what feels defensible.
sourceNotes: Original energy statement written for homemade.education. The release-and-allow framework is from The Money Zone (Rebecca Page, 2024).

content.text: An energy statement for the gap between the price you want to charge and the price you actually say. The release names the fear that sits in that gap; the allow names the alternative: pricing from the full value of the work, not from what feels least likely to cause objection.

I am ready to release the belief that my price must be acceptable to everyone, that lowering my rate makes me more likeable or more likely to get the work, or that undercharging is safer than charging what this is worth. I release it now. I release it now. I release it now.

I am ready to align with and allow a pricing practice that comes from the full worth of my work, not from the wobble, not from what I think they will accept, but from what this is genuinely worth. I allow it now. I allow it now. I allow it now.
Say each three times, slow, out loud or in your mind.
Written for homemade.education. The release-and-allow structure is from The Money Zone (Rebecca Page, 2024).
